title: SLERP: Spherical Linear Interpolation
concept: SLERP interpolates between model weights along a spherical path, preserving vector angles and producing smoother merges than simple linear averages.

exercises.prompt: In machine learning, what does 'interpolation' mean?

options.text: Blending or finding data points between two known values
Deleting weights to make a model smaller
Creating brand new data out of nothing

exercises.explain: Interpolation is the mathematical term for finding new data points between two known points, like blending two colors or two sets of model weights.

cards.title: The Secret of Smooth Merges
cards.body: Have you ever tried to mix two smart AI models together, only to end up with a confused robot that makes no sense? Simple averaging often breaks the delicate patterns inside neural networks. To merge them perfectly, we need to take a curved shortcut!

cards.title: What is SLERP?
cards.body: Spherical Linear Interpolation (SLERP) is a way to blend two vectors along a curved, circular path instead of a straight line. By rotating from one vector to another at a constant speed, it keeps the overall scale (magnitude) stable and prevents the merged model from losing its shape or knowledge.

cards.title: The Globe Walk
cards.body: Imagine you and a friend are on opposite sides of a giant globe. If you walked in a straight tunnel through the center of the Earth to reach them, you would sink into the hot core (which is like LERP's distorting straight line). Instead, you walk along the curved surface of the Earth to meet them. This smooth, curved walk is SLERP!

cards.title: Merging Personalities
cards.body: Imagine blending a 'Creative Writer' AI model with a 'Logical Programmer' AI model. If you use SLERP to merge their weights, you get a smooth transition that retains both creative storytelling and logical structure, without creating a broken, scrambled model in the middle.

cards.title: Is a Straight Line Always Best?
cards.body: Many people think that taking a simple average (LERP) of two model weights is the best way to merge them. But neural weights live on a high-dimensional sphere! Merging in a straight line cuts through the sphere, shrinking the weights and damaging the model's abilities. SLERP prevents this.

cards.title: Straight vs. Curved Paths
cards.body: Let's look at how LERP and SLERP travel between two points on a circle. Notice how the straight path shrinks in the middle, while the spherical path stays perfect.

right.text: Curves smoothly along the edge of the circle. The middle point maintains the exact same distance and energy.

left.text: Cuts straight through the circle. The middle point is closer to the center, shrinking the weights and losing information.

exercises.prompt: What is the main difference between SLERP and LERP?

options.text: SLERP travels along a curved spherical path, while LERP goes in a straight line.
SLERP only works on 2D images, while LERP works on all models.
SLERP is a simple average, while LERP requires complex calculus.
There is no difference; they are two names for the same math.

exercises.explain: LERP draws a straight line between two points, while SLERP rotates smoothly along a curved, spherical path.

exercises.prompt: True or False: SLERP helps preserve the scale and angle of model weights during a merge.
exercises.explain: SLERP is specifically designed to keep the length (magnitude) and angles of vectors consistent by moving along the surface of a sphere.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how SLERP blends vectors:
exercises.sentence: Unlike LERP which travels in a straight line, SLERP blends vectors along a curved path.
exercises.explain: SLERP moves along a curved spherical path to keep the length of the vectors the same, unlike a straight-line average.

exercises.prompt: Identify the correct AI-generated explanation of SLERP's purpose:

options.text: SLERP blends model weights by rotating along a sphere, keeping the vector length and details intact.
SLERP is a neural network optimizer that makes models run faster by reducing their weight sizes.
SLERP stands for Super Linear Extra Rotation Program, used to compile Python code.

exercises.explain: The first option is generated by an AI explaining SLERP simply. The others are incorrect or describe different concepts entirely.

exercises.prompt: Why is SLERP preferred over LERP when blending the neural weights of two complex AI models?

options.text: It is faster and uses less computing power than LERP.
It travels along a curved path to keep the vector length and angles stable.
It flattens the vectors into simple averages to speed up training.
It removes the circular features to make the model run on simpler hardware.

exercises.explain: SLERP is ideal for interpolating between deep learning weights (like model merges or latent vectors) because weights often sit on a high-dimensional sphere. Using a straight-line average (LERP) changes the scale/volume of the vectors, whereas SLERP rotates along the curve, keeping the underlying features and scale intact.
